title: centaurea iberica subsp. holzmanniana
intro: Centaurea iberica subsp. holzmanniana, also known as Holzman's knapweed, is a subspecies of the Centaurea iberica plant, a flowering perennial native to the Caucasus region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils. The health benefits of Centaurea iberica subsp. holzmanniana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Centaurea iberica subsp. holzmanniana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems.
